author: @ewjorgy
text:
One of the best pieces of advice I ever heard relating to how to solve a problem:  First come up with a solution.  Second, come up with a second, different solution.  

Often times the second solution is "better" because you remove the mental block of "that is the way it is always done" (i.e.- the first solution) from the equation. Now keep in mind that sometimes the first way actually IS better and it is always done that way because it has the most merit. To think that you are smarter than the millions of smart people that came before you is the height of arrogance, but every once in a while that second alternative solution IS better and you come away looking like a genius!  :)

Love your channel Rob!  All the best from California 💛

author: @kadmow
text:
Work with what you have - physically making art (or modifying precision components freehand - then  scanning into the system  - if a fantastic use of all levels to the industrial - progression.

I have been "into"  - as a curiosity, with some experimentation etc, all sorts to strange aerodynamic features like whale bumps, bird  "alulae" -leading edge devices -  wingtip modification (highly variable throughout the regimes  - then comes the other 3,:   variable camber, sweep and dihedral. - -They have us beat in the low Reynolds number arena (we need a lot more computing to compete...  Great to see the masses getting exposure from the  pre-Industrial ade to relativity.

Are these like vortex generators.. I guess im curious, What benefit the tubercles have over a planar wing surface? Is it something like a golf-ball's divots that give it extra lift and reduced noise? 

Im in the process of molding a carbon fiber propeller right now. I dont have a 3d printer or cnc machine so everything is craft mold for me. I was going to use a 2 part silicone mold. Place the blades in a vacuum heat seal food bag add the silicone then vacuum seal it. I figured that the vacuum bag would make a great dividing line for molding.

author: @danedmiston9673
text:
Tubercles looks like another form of turbulator.  A turbulator is a device that turns a laminar boundary layer into a turbulent boundary layer.  They are used on aircraft wings and large wind turbines to increase the angle of attack at which the wing stalls.
Fighter plane in WW2 used them to maintain control of the aircraft during extreme maneuvers.

In large wind turbines turbulators are used to keep the blades from stalling when strong gusts of wind hit the turbine.  They help so in getting the turbine rotor up to speed when starting up.  The trade off is that they slightly reduce the lift to drag ratio of the blades when operating at their designed tip speed ratio.
